(D) Local pubs are being seriously affected by gastropubs. First, I'll look at the article to find evidence for each option. The article starts by talking about pubs in the UK and the author's preference for pubs over bars because of their comfort and relaxed atmosphere. The author mentions that many people have favorite pubs near their homes or workplaces and that pubs are considered a cornerstone of British life. Then, the article discusses a change in the habit among some British people, where eating, rather than drinking beer, is becoming the main source of income for pubs. The gastropub is introduced as the reason for this shift. Gastropubs focus more on food and are more formal, often serving wine instead of traditional beer. The article also mentions that not everyone is happy with this trend. Some people dislike the fact that local pubs are closing down because gastropubs are more profitable. There's a quote from Rob Haward of the British Beer and Pub Association, stating that beer sales are sinking and many pubs are struggling to survive. Finally, the author concludes by saying they want to keep local British pubs alive and will visit them first when returning home.
